codeReview.summary: The offline indicator banner (VAL-UI-010) is cleanly implemented as a reusable OfflineBanner component that consumes SyncContext.isOnline. It is correctly integrated into all six required form pages (KasFormPage, BankFormPage, GudangFormPage, COAFormPage, BlokFormPage, AspekKerjaFormPage). The banner shows a clear Indonesian warning message, uses consistent Tailwind styling, conditionally renders only when offline, and does not block form functionality.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature F019-UI was assigned skillName 'backend-worker', but the work was purely frontend React/UI. The backend-worker SKILL.md describes database schema changes and main-process work with no UI procedure. The worker handoff reports followedProcedure=true with no deviations, which is misleading because the skill procedures were simply inapplicable. This suggests either the skill assignment should be more granular (e.g., 'frontend-worker' for UI features) or the handoff schema should capture skill mismatch.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows F019-UI skillName is 'backend-worker'. The backend-worker SKILL.md contains sections 'For Blok Database Changes' and 'For COA Database Changes' with no UI work procedure. Handoff for F019-UI reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true and deviations=[].

summary: Feature F019-UI passes review. The offline banner component meets all requirements of VAL-UI-010: it appears on all specified form pages, communicates that data will be saved locally and synced later, hides when online, and preserves full form usability. Typecheck, lint, and build succeeded per the handoff. No blocking or non-blocking code issues were found.
